stories.headline: Israel and Iran trade air strikes as ceasefire falters
stories.summary: Retaliatory barrages test 2-month truce and Donald Trump’s efforts to forge wider deal

stories.headline: China’s Xi lands in North Korea for Kim meeting
stories.summary: Chinese leader’s first trip to Pyongyang in 7 years comes as regime has stepped up rhetoric of nuclear expansion

stories.headline: Israel launches strikes on Iran after Tehran fires barrage of missiles
stories.summary: The exchange, after Tehran’s first such strike on Israel in two months, threatened to further complicate efforts to broker a peace deal aimed at ending the war.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Explosions in Tehran, Isfahan as Israel launches attacks
stories.summary: Escalation comes after Israel attacked Lebanon's capital, Beirut, killing at least two people and wounding 20.

stories.headline: Xi arrives in North Korea to meet Kim amid scrutiny of nuclear buildup
UK, France and Germany back direct talks with Russia 'with active US and European participation'
stories.summary: The leaders "supported the proposal for a direct dialogue between Ukraine and Russia, with active US and European participation, to bring about a ceasefire and support further negotiations", they said in a joint statement with Zelenskyy.

stories.headline: Strong earthquake strikes southern Philippines with tsunami warnings issued
stories.summary: People told to prepare for waves of up to three metres in parts of the Philippines, with smaller waves possible in Indonesia and MalaysiaA magnitude 7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early on Monday, sparking tsunami warnings on some regional coasts, collapsing buildings and killing at least three people.“Many buildings were affected, but I cannot enumerate them now because we are busy with ongoing rescues,” Master Sergeant Ro

stories.headline: Powerful earthquake hits Philippines, triggering tsunami alerts across Asia
stories.summary: Officials in Indonesia, the Philippines, and Japan warn of possible tsunami waves after an earthquake off Mindanao.

stories.headline: 7.8 magnitude earthquake rocks the Philippines, triggering tsunami warnings across the region
stories.summary: The strongest earthquake to hit the Philippines this year was centered at sea and caused damage in a key coastal city, knocking down power and setting off 1-metre tsunami waves along nearby coasts, officials said.

stories.headline: Software buyout deals collapse to lowest level since pandemic after AI rout
stories.summary: Value of private equity software acquisitions struck in the first five months of the year tumbles to $50bn

stories.headline: Flood Warnings for South China Farmlands Raise Rice-Crop Risks
stories.summary: Forecasts for heavy rains in southern China over the next week are raising the risk of flash floods, potentially inundating crops and damaging rice fields.

stories.headline: Copper Rebounds From Friday Slump on China-Buying and US Flows
stories.summary: Copper edged up to recoup some of last week’s losses as buying activity in China and flows of metal to the US supported the demand outlook.

stories.headline: Tech stocks plunge in Asia after record rally and renewed Middle East attacks
stories.summary: Markets in South Korea and Japan slid after a rally in tech stocks in recent weeks.

stories.headline: Tech sell-off widens as South Korea index plunges
stories.summary: Asian markets start week sharply lower after Nasdaq’s big Friday fall

stories.headline: Danantara to Start US Bond Roadshow as Indonesia Rout Deepens
stories.summary: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara is poised to kick off the US leg of its planned dollar bond roadshow this week, as the nation’s policymakers step up efforts to halt a deepening selloff in the nation’s markets.

stories.headline: Stocks Tumble as AI Trades Unwind | The Asia Trade 6/8/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Altitude Sickness Brings Stocks Down — Hard
stories.summary: A raft of IPOs led by Elon Musk’s SpaceX threatens to intensify the condition.

stories.headline: Saudis Lower July Oil Prices, Though Still at Decades High
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia cut the price of its main crude grade to Asia for a second straight month, though the premium for barrels to the kingdom’s largest market remained near the highest in decades.

stories.headline: Korean Stocks Tumble as Unwinding AI Trades Threaten Bull Run
stories.summary: South Korean stocks plunged, imperiling the world’s biggest rally, as investors pulled back from artificial intelligence bets that have fueled the global bull market in equities.

stories.headline: Indonesia’s Foreign Reserves Suffer Longest Decline Since 2018
stories.summary: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell for a fifth straight month in May, underscoring the cost of policymakers’ efforts to steady the rupiah after its slide to a record low.

stories.headline: Goldman’s Moe Sees Korea Stock Rebound After ‘Scary’ Correction
stories.summary: South Korean stocks are expected to bounce back after a drop that triggered a circuit breaker, said Timothy Moe, Goldman Sachs chief Asia-Pacific regional equity strategist.

stories.headline: Nithya Raman Overtakes Spencer Pratt in Race for L.A. Mayor
stories.summary: Nithya Raman pulled into second place in the race to see who will face Mayor Karen Bass in November. There are more votes to be counted.

stories.headline: Democrats in Congress Grapple With Concerns Over Platner Allegations
stories.summary: Representative Ro Khanna said that he believed the account of a woman who accused Graham Platner of physically threatening behavior, and urged his campaign not to criticize her.

stories.headline: The jailing of Germany’s most wanted woman – podcast
stories.summary: Dancer, dog owner, bank robber. Germany’s most wanted woman, Daniela Klette, has been sentenced to 13 years in prison after decades on the run. Deborah Cole and Jason Burke reportTo her friends and neighbours, there was nothing extraordinary about Claudia Ivone.As our Berlin correspondent, Deborah Cole, explains, the silver-haired 67-year-old had spent years living in the same apartment in a bohemian neighbourhood of West Berlin. She led an ordin

stories.headline: Peru’s Sanchez visits jailed ex-president as votes are counted
stories.summary: Peruvian presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez visited jailed former president Pedro Castillo.

stories.headline: Iran losing patience with Israel and US over ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Iran’s patience has reached its limit with the continuous violations of the ‘ceasefire’.

stories.headline: Russian drone hits building storing spent nuclear fuel near Chornobyl
stories.summary: Attack was ‘extremely vile’ and deliberate, says Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskyy A Russian Shahed drone has substantially damaged a building used to store spent nuclear fuel close to the disused Chornobyl nuclear power plant, in what Ukraine’s president described as a deliberate and “extremely vile” attack.While the structure – the reception building of the spent fuel storage facility – was empty of containers at the time, the targeting o

stories.headline: Trump says Netanyahu will have ‘no choice’ but to accept a deal with Iran
stories.summary: US president tells FT that he ‘calls the shots’ after urging the Israeli leader not to retaliate against Tehran

stories.headline: Trump Defends Compensation Fund and Iran War in ‘Meet the Press’ Interview
stories.summary: In a lengthy interview with NBC’s “Meet the Press,” the president again vowed that gas prices would go down when the war in Iran ends.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping set to meet Kim Jong-un in North Korea, as China seeks to revitalise relationship
stories.summary: The China-North Korea relationship has been strained by a fall in trade during the pandemic and Pyongyang’s increasing ties with RussiaXi Jinping visits North Korea on Monday for a two-day trip, his first in nearly seven years, as China’s president looks to revitalise ties with his junior ally.Xi is expected to meet North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, in Pyongyang. North Korea is China’s only formal treaty ally but in recent years their relationsh

stories.headline: Trump says will not unfreeze Iranian assets before ceasefire deal reached
stories.summary: Iranian officials have indicated the release of funds would build 'trust' needed to reach lasting deal ending war.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy discusses ‘urgent need to scale up’ air defences with key allies in London
stories.summary: Keir Starmer hosts Ukrainian, French and German leaders in Downing Street after Russia fires hypersonic weapons at UkraineVolodymyr Zelenskyy and the leaders of the UK, France and Germany discussed “the urgent need to scale up” Ukraine’s air defences and deep-strike capabilities in London on Sunday night, after Russia fired hypersonic weapons at Ukraine, Downing Street said.The meeting of Ukraine’s staunchest allies in London came hours after a R

stories.headline: Hegseth attacks Europe over migration with beach 'invasion' D-Day speech
stories.summary: The US defence secretary was speaking in Normandy, 82 years after allied forces launched their operation to liberate Nazi-occupied north-western Europe.

stories.headline: Hegseth Criticizes Europe Over Migration ‘Invasion’ in D-Day Speech
stories.summary: The remarks from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reflect many of the Trump administration’s previous assertions on immigrants in Europe, which overlap with the language of European far-right political parties.

stories.headline: Trump walks out of ‘Meet the Press’ interview when challenged over false claims
stories.summary: When pressed by host Kristen Welker, the president cited no evidence for claims about Jan. 6 and elections he said were “rigged.”

stories.headline: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
stories.summary: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Trump abruptly ends NBC interview after clash over 'rigged election' claim
stories.summary: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.

stories.headline: Eriksen conscious after collapsing in Denmark game
stories.summary: Christian Eriksen collapsed during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine but is now conscious, says the Danish Football Association.

stories.headline: Robert Coles, Pulitzer-Winning Child Psychiatrist, Is Dead at 97
stories.summary: His five-volume “Children of Crisis” series, published between 1967 and 1977, drew on his conversations with American children whose voices were not often heard.

stories.headline: Lawsuit Aims to Stop U.F.C. Fights at White House on Trump’s Birthday
stories.summary: A federal lawsuit said the event, set for June 14, was unlawfully planned and designed to benefit Mr. Trump and his allies.

stories.headline: Lawsuit seeks to stop Trump’s planned White House UFC match
stories.summary: Legal challenge claims US president did not seek proper approval for fighting event to be held on his 80th birthday.

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

stories.headline: Armenia votes as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
stories.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

stories.headline: Indonesia Market Rout Deepens as Bonds, Currency, Stocks Slide
stories.summary: The rout in Indonesian markets deepened on Monday with the nation’s bonds, stocks and currency tumbling.

stories.headline: Japan’s Nikkei Slumps as Tech Selloff, Iran Tensions Deepen
stories.summary: Japanese stocks fell by the most since March as AI shares retreated from their highs amid rising expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep rates higher for longer following strong US jobs data.

stories.headline: RFK Jr. Appears Disengaged on Many Health Department Matters Beyond Vaccines
stories.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has demonstrated little interest in managing his sprawling department as he focuses on food and vaccine policies, according to colleagues.

stories.headline: ‘Ugly in a beautiful way’: Denmark’s mullet championship celebrates divisive hairstyle
stories.summary: Danish follicle rebels go head to head in competition for best short-in-the-front, long-in-the-back cutBusiness in the front, party in the back. A packed Danish crowd has celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle, defined by very short hair at the front and longer hair at the back.Denmark’s raucous 2026 Mullet Championship, presented on an outdoor stage in central Copenhagen, attracted 12 well-coiffed competitors and more than 1,

stories.headline: Hezbollah MP: Resistance defends Lebanon where the state failed
stories.summary: Ibrahim Moussawi says Hezbollah stepped in after diplomacy and the Lebanese state failed.

stories.headline: Bolivia’s legislature passes law allowing use of troops against protesters
stories.summary: New law grants president power to use military to clear roadblocks set up amid weeks of antigovernment demonstrations.

stories.headline: NATO drills: France to test AI battlefield tech as alternative to US system
stories.summary: Arcadia, developed with French firms, is a European response to Maven, an AI command and control system used by NATO that was developed by the US company Palantir.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
stories.summary: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

stories.headline: Ebola spread in central Africa could match 2014 record outbreak, US health officials say
stories.summary: Modelling from US CDC shows Ebola spread could be on ‘dangerous trajectory’, but experts warn outbreaks can be very hard to predictCentral Africa’s Ebola outbreak could spread to be similar in scale to the worst outbreak in history, west Africa’s 2014-2016 outbreak that killed more than 11,000 people, according to a new analysis by US health officials.The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) on Friday published a range of scenarios

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: On China, Trump picked the right battle but the wrong strategy
stories.summary: A long trade war looms. Trump’s scattershot protectionism, chaotic tariffs and belligerence against our natural allies guarantees that US trade policy will remain a hot messWe are in for a long trade war.In the months since “Liberation Day” last year, when Donald Trump let loose a volley of tariffs against imports from everywhere, countries have rushed to build new relationships in the hope of maybe circumventing the US to protect the global trad

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on
stories.summary: Fifty years after his debut, Ilaiyaraaja remains one of the most celebrated composers in Indian cinema.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping to meet Kim Jong Un in rare visit to North Korea
stories.summary: It is taking place weeks after Xi met the leaders of the US and Russia - two countries that loom large over North Korea's foreign policy.

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan
stories.summary: More than 1 million people advised to evacuate homes amid 80mph winds and heavy rainTyphoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) moved northwards over the course of this week. From Okinawa to mainland Japan, prolonged and heavy rainfall led to landslide warnings and the flooding of rivers, with Japan issuing level 4 warnings for some rivers, signalling a risk of overflowing. This level is high enough for municipalities to issue evacuation orders.

stories.headline: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says
stories.summary: Mette-Marit's condition, which stiffens the lungs, making it hard to breathe, has deteriorated, the royal household says.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 11 people in Gaza City, medics say
stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed in the attacks on four residential buildings, which Israel's military says killed four Hamas security officials.

stories.headline: Bondi Beach shooting hero charged with assault
stories.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed disarmed one of the alleged shooters during the deadly attack at a Jewish event.

stories.headline: Delcy Rodríguez visits India: Will oil talks lead to an energy deal?
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez's India visit highlights Venezuela's growing role in Delhi's oil diversification.

stories.headline: Protesters topple World Cup player statues in Mexico City
stories.summary: Teachers demanding higher pay have been demonstrating for several days and threaten to disrupt the tournament if their demands are not met.

stories.headline: Civilians flee as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire in Mogadishu
stories.summary: Violence flares before protests on Thursday over president’s decision to remain in office after his term expiredFierce clashes have taken place between government troops and militias allied with the opposition in Somalia’s capital, Mogadishu, damaging property and forcing some civilians to flee.In the runup to the fighting, which started on Wednesday afternoon, opposition leaders embedded with militias set up positions in their clan strongholds t

stories.headline: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales
stories.summary: Beluga whales, which Marineland threatened to euthanize in 2025, will be moved to aquariums in Spain or across USCanada and an embattled marine park have reached a tentative deal on the future of 30 beluga whales, ending a saga that has captivated the public and angered animal rights groups.The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US, endin

stories.headline: 'Crazy' phone call between Trump and Netanyahu complicates Iran talks
stories.summary: Israel's PM laughed off reports of friction, but he has tested the patience of other US presidents.

stories.headline: Goldman’s Japan Chief Counts on New Leaders for Growth
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Japan president Hidehiro Imatsu is promoting younger executives at the firm as a merger boom, rising stocks and volatile bond yields fuel the most intense talent war in years.

stories.headline: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
stories.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

stories.headline: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.headline: American capitalism has taken an apocalyptic turn
stories.summary: Millenarian thinking permeates business and markets

stories.headline: Video shows elaborate drug-smuggling tunnel between US and Mexico
stories.summary: Four people have been arrested and charged with trafficking more than $45 million in cocaine, according to federal officials.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.headline: Iran attacks damage 20 US military sites since start of war, satellite images show
stories.summary: Analysts told BBC Verify that Tehran's strikes were more extensive than publicly acknowledged.
